perturbation.original_text: HDI shall design, develop, implement, operate, maintain and manage, and enable the Company to establish a presence on the World Wide Web ("Site") to make available to Internet users on demand, men's and women's apparel and accessories from the Deerskin Catalog ("Deerskin Products"). As used in this Agreement, "Deerskin Products" shall not include (i) closeout merchandise which the Company may identify as "Deerskin" branded items, if such is the case, nor (ii) products from the Company's Joan Cook Catalog.
perturbation.changed_text: HDI shall design, develop, implement, operate, maintain and manage, and enable the Company to establish a presence on the World Wide Web ("Platform") to make available to Internet users on demand, men's and women's apparel and accessories from the Deerskin Catalog ("Deerskin Goods"). As used in this Agreement, "Deerskin Goods" shall not include (i) closeout merchandise which the Company may identify as "Deerskin" branded items, if such is the case, nor (ii) products from the Company's Joan Cook Catalog.
perturbation.explanation: The terms 'Site' is being replaced by 'Platform' and the term 'Deerskin Products' is being replaced by 'Deerskin Goods'. This substitution introduces confusion because throughout the contract, both terms are used interchangeably without defining that those terms represent the same concepts, leading to uncertainty in the obligations related to the website and the products being sold. Throughout the document, the term 'Site' is still being used which leads to a direct in-text contradiction, regarding its responsabilities.
perturbation.contradicted_text: HDI shall host and maintain the Site on a server provided by HDI.
perturbation.justification: The modification replaces 'Site' with 'Platform' and 'Deerskin Products' with 'Deerskin Goods'. Since the contradicted text refers to 'Site', the change introduces a contradiction because the contract now uses both terms interchangeably without a clear definition, leading to confusion about which term applies and where responsibilities lie. This is a Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction, as the interchangeably used terms create conflicting obligations regarding HDI's responsibilities for hosting and maintaining either the 'Site' or the 'Platform'.

perturbation.original_text: The Company shall pay HDI thirty percent (3016) of the Net Sales in excess of Eleven Thousand Dollars ($11,000) per calendar month. "Net Sales" shall mean all revenues from the sale of Deerskin Products on the Site including shipping and handling charges, minus refunds and exchanges.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company shall pay HDI thirty percent (3016) of the Net Sales in excess of Eleven Thousand Dollars ($11,000) per calendar month. "Net Sales" shall mean all revenues from the sale of Deerskin Items on the Platform including shipping and handling charges, minus returns and exchanges.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Deerskin Products' with 'Deerskin Items', and 'refunds' with 'returns', creates ambiguity. While seemingly synonymous, these terms can be interpreted differently. Especially given the first in-text contradiction perturbation which modifies the original 'site' definition. Returns could be interpreted more broadly than refunds and the contract does not explicitly address if those terms are interchangable, leading to disputes over the calculation of 'Net Sales' and the applicable payment amount, specially with the first in-text contradiction being made.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Quarterly reconciliation of payments shall be conducted within thirty (30) days of the end of each calendar quarter to adjust for refunds and exchanges not taken into account in payments made to HDI.
perturbation.justification: The modified text changes 'Deerskin Products' to 'Deerskin Items' and 'refunds' to 'returns'. This creates a 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' because these terms are used interchangeably without clear definition. The change introduces ambiguity in calculating 'Net Sales' as the scope of 'returns' compared to 'refunds' isn't defined, directly conflicting with the 'Contradicted Text' that specifies 'refunds and exchanges' for quarterly reconciliation, which leads to a disagreement in calculating payment. The explanation clearly highlights this contradiction based on the lack of explicit definitions.

perturbation.original_text: Each of HDI and the Company shall appoint a -5- General Manager of its own to act as liaison with the other Party for the Site (each a "General Manager") who shall bear sole responsibility for bookkeeping and business operations of the Site on a day-today basis. Each General Manager shall have the authority to make and convey decisions on behalf of each Party and to be the liaison with the other Party for all production and Content matters.
perturbation.changed_text: Each of HDI and the Company shall appoint a -5- Administrator of its own to act as liaison with the other Party for the Platform (each an "Administrator") who shall bear sole responsibility for bookkeeping and business operations of the Web Space on a day-today basis. Each Administrator shall have the authority to make and convey decisions on behalf of each Party and to be the liaison with the other Party for all production and Content matters.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'General Manager' to 'Administrator', 'Site' to 'Platform', and 'Site' to 'Web Space' introduces role and scope confusion. The contract refers to 'General Manager' in section 3.4, and 'Site Management' in section 5. This change creates an in-text contradiction because it's unclear if the responsibilities previously assigned to the 'General Manager' and 'Site' still apply to the 'Administrator' and 'Platform' or 'Web Space', respectively, particularly concerning financial oversight.
perturbation.contradicted_text: HDI's General Manager (as hereinafter defined), may upon no less than thirty (30) days prior written notice to the Company, have the right to inspect the records of the Company's General Manager reasonably related to the calculation of such payments during the Company's normal business hours. The fees incurred by HDI in connection with the inspection shall be borne by HDI.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'General Manager' to 'Administrator' and 'Site' to 'Platform' or 'Web Space' directly contradicts the section referencing 'HDI's General Manager' and their rights regarding inspection of records. The changed text creates uncertainty about who holds these responsibilities and rights, leading to a direct conflict in the application of the contract. The original contract clearly defined the role of the 'General Manager' with specific responsibilities, while the changed text introduces a different title, potentially invalidating the provisions tied to the 'General Manager'. This falls under 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' as the change introduces conflicting obligations.
